records.ety: [OE. forfenden; pref. for- + fenden to fend. See Fend, v. t.]

defs: To hinder; to fend off; to avert; to prevent the approach of; to forbid or prohibit. See Forfend.
quotes: God forefend it should ever be recorded in our history.
It would be a far better work . . . to forefend the cruelty.
